parameters.content:
## What it is ? [ WEEKLY AUTOMATED REPORTING SYSTEM 🧠 ]

### 📊 An automated system that monitors the team's Jira project every Monday morning, measures progress against the active sprint, and delivers a structured report to stakeholders — with no manual effort required.


## What problem it solves ?

### Sprint progress is invisible until someone manually compiles it. By then it is too late to act. Blocked work goes unnoticed. Overdue issues accumulate quietly. Team capacity is unbalanced without anyone realising. This system makes sprint health visible every week, automatically, before problems escalate.


## How it works ?

### Every Monday at ??:?? the system runs automatically. It pulls live data from Jira, measures what happened over the past 7 days, and produces three outputs simultaneously:

- An **HTML report**  + **PDF Attachement** sent by email to the team
- A **Markdown version** for documentation and archiving
- A set of **structured records** saved to a database for historical analysis

> ### The entire process takes under one minute and requires no human input.


## Multi-Project support

### The system runs one instance per Jira project. All instances share the same database, so cross-project analysis is available out of the box. Adding a new project requires changing a single configuration value (**CONFIGURATION NODE**) — everything else adapts automatically


## Historical Analysis

### Every report is saved to a project shared database. Over time this builds a searchable record that answers questions no single report can:

- Is the team's velocity improving sprint over sprint?
- Is lead time decreasing as the team matures?
- Which team member consistently resolves issues fastest?
- Which epics have stalled across multiple weeks?
- How does this project compare to others running in parallel?

> ### This turns weekly snapshots into a continuous picture of team performance


## JIRA SPACE CONFIGURATION COMPATIBILITY

### Link to Documentation: https://www.notion.so/JIRA-COMPATIBILITY-GUIDE-335f6ec488f68037853adb1722711cae?source=copy_link 🔎


## 📩 Contact / Questions / Custom Requests

### Workflow created by
**aixautomation**  
- Email: aixautomation01@gmail.com  
- Website: https://www.aixautomation.tech  

### Need help?
If you have any questions about this workflow, need support setting it up, or want to request a custom workflow, feel free to get in touch.
## PREPARE DATA ( DATA CONFIGURATION / JIRA ISSUES ) 🤖
## CREATE TABLES FOR FIRST TIME RUNNING WORKFLOW 1️⃣
## STORE IN DATABASE FOR LATER ANALYTICS WITH (PowerBI, MetaBase...) 📊

## PROJECT CONFIG — change only this NODE 
### Variables to change:
(**Project Key**, **Jira Domain**, **Email To**)

parameters.query:
-- Drop old unique constraints and replace with multi-project safe ones

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sprint_reports (
  id                  SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  project_key         TEXT NOT NULL,
  project_name        TEXT,               -- full name: "PFE Project: Agent IA..."
  project_short       TEXT,               -- display: "Agent IA Backlog..."
  sprint_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
  week_start          DATE NOT NULL,
  week_end            DATE NOT NULL,
  sprint_start        DATE,
  sprint_end          DATE,
  sprint_pct_elapsed  INT,
  sprint_goal         TEXT,
  total_issues        INT,
  completed_this_week INT,
  updated_this_week   INT,
  created_this_week   INT,
  due_soon            INT,
  html_report         TEXT,
  markdown_report     TEXT,
  subject_line        TEXT,
  status_overview     JSONB,
  priority_breakdown  JSONB,
  due_soon_issues     JSONB,
  created_at          TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),

  -- scoped to project + sprint + week (a sprint can span multiple weeks)
  UNIQUE (project_key, sprint_name, week_start)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS weekly_kpis (
  id                     SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  report_id              INT REFERENCES sprint_reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  project_key            TEXT NOT NULL,
  sprint_name            TEXT NOT NULL,
  week_start             DATE NOT NULL,
  -- velocity
  story_points_completed INT  DEFAULT 0,
  story_points_total     INT  DEFAULT 0,
  story_points_remaining INT  DEFAULT 0,
  -- completion
  completion_rate        INT  DEFAULT 0,   -- %
  throughput_week        INT  DEFAULT 0,
  throughput_sprint      INT  DEFAULT 0,
  -- health
  sprint_health          TEXT,             -- 'On Track' | 'Slight Risk' | 'At Risk' | 'Critical'
  health_gap             INT  DEFAULT 0,   -- pct points behind target
  -- lead time
  avg_lead_time          NUMERIC(5,1),     -- null = no resolved issues
  min_lead_time          NUMERIC(5,1),
  max_lead_time          NUMERIC(5,1),
  -- counts
  done_count             INT  DEFAULT 0,
  in_progress_count      INT  DEFAULT 0,
  todo_count             INT  DEFAULT 0,
  blocked_count          INT  DEFAULT 0,
  overdue_count          INT  DEFAULT 0,
  -- sub-arrays stored as jsonb (not useful to aggregate row-by-row)
  lead_time_by_priority  JSONB,
  blocked_list           JSONB,
  overdue_list           JSONB,
  created_at             TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),

  UNIQUE (project_key, sprint_name, week_start)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS assignee_kpis (
  id                  SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  report_id           INT REFERENCES sprint_reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  project_key         TEXT NOT NULL,
  project_short       TEXT,
  sprint_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
  week_start          DATE NOT NULL,
  assignee            TEXT NOT NULL,
  total_issues        INT  DEFAULT 0,
  done                INT  DEFAULT 0,
  in_progress         INT  DEFAULT 0,
  todo                INT  DEFAULT 0,
  blocked             INT  DEFAULT 0,
  story_points_done   INT  DEFAULT 0,
  story_points_total  INT  DEFAULT 0,
  avg_lead_time       NUMERIC(5,1),
  completion_rate     INT  DEFAULT 0,
  created_at          TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),

  UNIQUE (project_key, sprint_name, week_start, assignee)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS epic_snapshots (
  id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  report_id       INT REFERENCES sprint_reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  project_key     TEXT NOT NULL,
  sprint_name     TEXT NOT NULL,
  week_start      DATE NOT NULL,
  epic_key        TEXT NOT NULL,
  epic_name       TEXT,
  total_issues    INT  DEFAULT 0,
  done_pct        INT  DEFAULT 0,
  in_progress_pct INT  DEFAULT 0,
  todo_pct        INT  DEFAULT 0,
  created_at      TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),

  UNIQUE (project_key, sprint_name, week_start, epic_key)
);

-- Indexes covering every query pattern to run
CREATE INDEX ON sprint_reports (project_key, week_start); -- SELECT * FROM sprint_reports WHERE project_key='AIBAP' AND week_start='2026-03-30';
CREATE INDEX ON sprint_reports (sprint_name); -- SELECT * FROM sprint_reports WHERE sprint_name='Sprint 24';
CREATE INDEX ON weekly_kpis   (project_key, week_start); -- SELECT * FROM weekly_kpis WHERE project_key='AIBAP' AND week_start='2026-03-30';
CREATE INDEX ON weekly_kpis   (sprint_name);
CREATE INDEX ON assignee_kpis (project_key, assignee, week_start);
CREATE INDEX ON assignee_kpis (assignee);  -- cross-project: "show me assignee across all projects", SELECT * FROM assignee_kpis WHERE assignee='John';
CREATE INDEX ON epic_snapshots(project_key, epic_key, week_start); -- SELECT * FROM epic_snapshots WHERE project_key='AIBAP' AND epic_key='EPIC-123' AND week_start='2026-03-30';

options.jql: =project = "{{ $input.first().json.projectKey }}" AND sprint in openSprints() AND updated >= -7d ORDER BY updated DESC
=project = "{{ $input.first().json.projectKey }}" AND sprint in openSprints() AND created >= -7d
=project = "{{ $input.first().json.projectKey }}" AND sprint in openSprints() AND statusCategory in ("Done") AND updated >= -7d
=project = "{{ $input.first().json.projectKey }}" AND sprint in openSprints() AND duedate >= now() AND duedate <= endOfDay("+7d") AND statusCategory != "Done"

parameters.jsCode:
// Prepare Sprint Report row — reads purely from $input
const d = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: {
    project_key:         d.projectKey,
    project_name:        d.projectName,
    project_short:       d.projectShort,
    sprint_name:         d.sprintName,
    sprint_goal:         d.sprintGoal         || null,
    sprintStartFmt:      d.sprintStartFmt,
    sprintEndFmt:        d.sprintEndFmt,
    week_start:          d.weekStart,
    week_end:            d.weekEnd,
    sprint_pct_elapsed:  d.sprintPct,
    total_issues:        d.metrics?.totalIssues        ?? 0,
    completed_this_week: d.metrics?.completed          ?? 0,
    updated_this_week:   d.metrics?.updated            ?? 0,
    created_this_week:   d.metrics?.created            ?? 0,
    due_soon:            d.metrics?.dueSoon            ?? 0,
    html_report:         d.html                        || null,
    markdown_report:     d.markdown                    || null,
    subject_line:        d.subject                     || null,
    status_overview:     JSON.stringify(d.statusOverview    || []),
    priority_breakdown:  JSON.stringify(d.priorityBreakdown || []),
    due_soon_issues:     JSON.stringify(d.dueSoonIssues     || []),
}}];

// Prepare Weekly KPIs row — reads purely from $input
const d = $input.first().json;
const k = d.kpis || {};
return [{ json: {
    report_id:               d.id,
    project_key:             d.project_key,
    sprint_name:             d.sprint_name,
    week_start:              d.week_start,
    story_points_completed:  k.storyPointsCompleted  ?? 0,
    story_points_total:      k.storyPointsTotal      ?? 0,
    story_points_remaining:  k.storyPointsRemaining  ?? 0,
    completion_rate:         k.completionRate        ?? 0,
    throughput_week:         k.throughputWeek        ?? 0,
    throughput_sprint:       k.throughputSprint      ?? 0,
    sprint_health:           k.sprintHealth          || null,
    health_gap:              k.healthGap             ?? 0,
    avg_lead_time:           k.avgLeadTime           ?? null,
    min_lead_time:           k.minLeadTime           ?? null,
    max_lead_time:           k.maxLeadTime           ?? null,
    done_count:              k.doneCount             ?? 0,
    in_progress_count:       k.inProgressCount       ?? 0,
    todo_count:              k.toDoCount             ?? 0,
    blocked_count:           k.blockedCount          ?? 0,
    overdue_count:           k.overdueCount          ?? 0,
    lead_time_by_priority:   JSON.stringify(k.leadTimeByPriority || []),
    blocked_list:            JSON.stringify(k.blockedList        || []),
    overdue_list:            JSON.stringify(k.overdueList        || []),
}}];

// Build Final Payload
// Combine Outputs passes 1 merged item containing everything.
const d = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: {
    projectKey:    d.projectKey,
    projectName:   d.projectName,
    projectShort:  d.projectShort,
    emailTo:       d.emailTo,

    weekStart:     d.weekStart,
    weekEnd:       d.weekEnd,
    sprintName:    d.sprintName,
    sprintGoal:    d.sprintGoal,
    sprintDates:   d.sprintDates,
    sprintStartFmt:d.sprintStartFmt,
    sprintEndFmt:  d.sprintEndFmt,
    sprintPct:     d.sprintPct,
    sprintDaysLeft:d.sprintDaysLeft,
    reportTitle:   d.reportTitle,
    dateRange:     d.dateRange,
    weekLabel:     d.weekLabel,
    today:         d.today,
    metrics:           d.metrics,
    kpis:              d.kpis,
    statusOverview:    d.statusOverview,
    priorityBreakdown: d.priorityBreakdown,
    teamWorkload:      d.teamWorkload,
    epicProgress:      d.epicProgress,
    recentActivity:    d.recentActivity,
    dueSoonIssues:     d.dueSoonIssues,
    html:         d.html       || null,
    subject:      d.subject    || null,
    fullHtml:     d.fullHtml   || null,
    markdown:     d.markdown   || null,
    mdFilename:   d.filename   || d.mdFilename || null,
}}];
